title: Jamaican Stir Fry
description: A spicy and tender Jamaican-inspired tempeh stir fry that's perfect for a quick and comforting dinner.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onion and bell pepper and stir-fry until they start to soften, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the tempeh and continue to stir-fry until it's golden brown and crispy, about 4-5 minutes.
Add the hot sauce, soy sauce, and honey, and stir-fry for another minute.
Add the scotch bonnet pepper (if using) and stir-fry for another minute, until the flavors have melded together.
Serve the stir-fry hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro or scallions (if desired).
Note: If using scotch bonnet peppers, be careful when handling them as the oils in the peppers can cause skin irritation.
Cook the stir-fry over medium-high heat, stirring constantly, to prevent the tempeh from burning.
Adjust the amount of hot sauce and scotch bonnet peppers to taste, depending on your desired level of heat.
